test.command: MILESTONE="m2-context-pack-ui" bash -lc 'if [ -n "${MILESTONE:-}" ]; then ./.factory/library/verify-flow-report-finalization.sh "$MILESTONE" || exit 1; fi; scripts/test-openburnbar-swift.sh && scripts/test-openburnbar-app.sh'

blockingIssues.description: `AgentLens/Views/Dashboard/ContextPackSheet.swift` still treats `anchorSessionId` as a single-conversation fetch and ignores `anchorProject`, so Session Detail launches produce a one-session export instead of the required project-scoped pack (VAL-CTXDETAIL-005 / VAL-CTXCROSS-007).

`AgentLens/Views/Dashboard/SessionDetailView.swift` overwrites `conversation` and `summaryText` after the async lookup without clearing prior state first, which can leak stale prior-session data during rapid switches/reanchor and violates the no-stale-state requirement (VAL-CTXDETAIL-010 / VAL-CTXDETAIL-007).

`AgentLensTests/Active/ContextPackSessionDetailSurfaceTests.swift` renamed the contract validator methods and remains data-layer-only, so the required `xcodebuild -only-testing` entry points will miss the intended SwiftUI visibility/order/tap-routing assertions.

`AgentLensTests/Active/ContextPackCrossFlowTests.swift` exercises `ContextPackService.assemble` directly instead of launching `ContextPackSheet` through `DashboardView` and `SessionDetailView`, so the actual cross-entry wiring is still unverified.

The cross-flow parity assertions are too weak: they only check counts/presence/markers and do not enforce ordered session-id equality or normalized shared-body equality, leaving same-anchor and envelope-only regressions possible.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Require Context Pack UI workers to keep the validation-contract validator names verbatim and to assert the actual Dashboard/Session Detail/CrossFlow SwiftUI entrypoints instead of accepting renamed or service-layer-only tests.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The session-detail suite renamed contract methods and stayed data-layer only, while the cross-flow suite called `ContextPackService.assemble` directly and never instantiated `DashboardView`, `SessionDetailView`, or `ContextPackSheet`.
